subject: LU-12501 utils: stop showdf() endless loop

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

That makes sense, but I'm puzzled as to why this ever completes...  Maybe we don't have anything reporting non-rotational.  Oh dear.

The link was in the previous comment, but I'll repeat it here:

 > I pushed a version of this fix ... in patch
 > https://review.whamcloud.com/35456 "LU-12501 utils: fix 'lfs df' printing loop"

which has passed testing and is now ready for inspection, so I'm going to mark this patch -1.

Abandoned

This patch did a single fix but Andreas has a patch the fixes many broken things.

commit.subject: LU-12501 utils: stop showdf() endless loop
commit.message:
LU-12501 utils: stop showdf() endless loop

The function showdf() in lfs.c copies the struct obd_statfs
os_state field and then loops over each bit to test if it is
set. If the bit is set then its masked out. The loop then exit
once all the bits are masked out. This does not happen for the
OS_STAT_NONROT case. Since the OS_STAT_NONROT is never masked
the loop never breaks. Always mask out OS_STAT_NONROT instead
which ensures we still skip this value and allows all bits to
be masked to break the loop.

Change-Id: Ib62e949ca56d691c4699d5f2d9439c4264405fc2
Fixes: 68635c3d9b3 ("LU-11963 osd: Add nonrotational flag to statfs")
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
